mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a robust labor market and strong economic fundamentals, which supports corporate earnings and investor confidence. This sustained strength in the labor market is expected to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% is historically low as of 2023-08
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods where UNRATE is below 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is high, it indicates persistent pricing pressures and a restrictive monetary policy stance. This combination leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability expectations, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where CPIAUCSL is above 300 and FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a broad-based weakening of consumer demand and investment. This indicates an impending economic slowdown or recession, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where both retail sales and housing starts show significant declines over a 10-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic expansion, increasing industrial production (INDPRO) combined with positive GDP growth (GDPC1) signals strong manufacturing activity and overall economic health. This environment supports corporate profitability and leads to positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month % change > 0.5% AND GDPC1 year-over-year growth > 1.0%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where INDPRO is growing and GDPC1 growth is positive during an expansionary regime, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes or accompanies economic recessions. This leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% is currently low, but a sharp rise would be a significant negative signal
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances following a significant increase in UNRATE over a 10-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a supportive environment for corporate earnings and equity valuations. This underlying economic strength drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20386.467 indicates a large and growing economy as of 2023-04
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods when GDPC1 shows positive year-over-year growth over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a tight labor market (low UNRATE) creates a challenging environment for the Federal Reserve, forcing them to maintain restrictive monetary policy (high FEDFUNDS). This leads to higher interest rates and a negative outlook for equities.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 45% of periods where CPIAUCSL is above 305, UNRATE is below 4.0%, and FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% over a 7-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job creation (PAYEMS) combined with robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicates healthy consumer demand and economic activity. This positive momentum supports corporate revenue growth and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 100000 AND RSAFS 3-month % change > 0.5%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances where both PAYEMS and RSAFS show strong growth over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.
